T-B8-003a adds a public-QASM packet spoofer boundary: public parser/emulator prediction success is 1.0 across all 36 packet circuits, so public-packet protocol soundness is rejected and private late-binding is required. T-B8-003b adds a late-bound private challenge contract: 36 public skeletons remove verifier-private masks/flips, but deterministic public data transcripts remain classically predictable, so late-bound parity challenges alone are insufficient. T-B8-003c adds a non-stabilizer late-bound transcript pilot: all 36 public skeletons receive H plus T/RZ(pi/4) challenge-basis layers, the deterministic transcript blocker is removed for 36/36 pilot circuits, minimum min-entropy is 4 bits, and maximum output probability is 0.0625; it remains an exact small-probability pilot, not hardware execution, cryptographic soundness, sampling hardness, quantum advantage, or BQP separation. T-B8-003d adds a support-aware spoofer gate: four public-support spoofer families attack all 36 non-stabilizer pilot circuits; exact transcript success remains capped at 0.0625, but support-only verifier acceptance is 1.0, so support-membership soundness is rejected. T-B8-003e adds a verifier-private predicate pressure gate: four private bits reduce support-aware spoofer acceptance from 1.0 to 0.0625 under no leakage, while one-bit leakage gives 0.125 and full leakage gives 1.0. T-B4-002b/T-B8-003f now adds a formal verifier-private commit-challenge-response-verify protocol model over the same 36 B4/B8 rows: 4 private predicate bits, 4 protocol rounds, 8/8 analytic gates passed, public support-only acceptance 0.5, hidden-private acceptance 0.0625, one-bit leakage acceptance 0.125, three-bit leakage acceptance 0.5, and full private-material leakage acceptance 1.0. It is a formal protocol simulation and leakage cascade, not hardware execution, cryptographic soundness, protocol soundness, sampling hardness, quantum advantage, or BQP separation.
dossiers.current_difficulty: Easy-to-verify tasks can be easy to fake; hard-to-simulate tasks can be hard to verify; noise and adaptive spoofers narrow soundness.
dossiers.lineage: Interactive proofs, blind quantum computation, sampling advantage, and classical verification; representative milestones include UBQC and Mahadev verification.
dossiers.our_solution: Hidden-trap and hidden-invariant hybrid tasks with leakage stress tests, challenge refresh, and circuit-derived projection checks.
dossiers.remaining: The formal verifier-private protocol remains an analytic model. It still needs noise-modeled or real-backend transcript execution, hardware randomized-measurement verifier evidence, cryptographic or protocol-soundness proof assumptions if claimed, and stronger learned/generative spoofer attacks under explicit leakage.
remaining_steps: Run the formal private challenge protocol through Qiskit Aer/noise-modeled transcript bridges.
Replace GenericBackendV2-style assumptions with real backend properties or hardware randomized-measurement execution.
Attack the private protocol with learned/generative spoofers under no-leak, partial-leak, and full-leak regimes.
State a theorem-level completeness/soundness target only after the empirical protocol survives stronger attacks.

dossiers.completed: Toy hidden-invariant verifier with honest completeness 1.0; five adversaries rejected; 48-configuration leakage test shows 0.75 leakage dangerous; toy refresh/rotation repair passes the <=5% high-leakage soundness gate; shared B4/B8 circuit-refresh proxy reduces high-leakage adaptive soundness from 0.675 without refresh to 0.0 under repaired modes; trained/generative spoofer stress runs 144 configs, reaches learned soundness 1.0 without refresh, and keeps projection_rotation/challenge_refresh/refresh_plus_rotation <=5% in this proxy; B10-T2 proof-obligation gate rejects no-refresh high-leakage claims; restricted refresh-independence lemma gives a 8.94e-44 single-unknown-mask bound under declared assumptions; transcript leakage simulator runs 192 configs and shows refreshed high-leakage modes retain >=6 unknown independent predicates with max empirical soundness 0.025 while no-refresh high leakage remains unsafe; device-noise transcript bridge runs 480 configs and keeps challenge_refresh/refresh_plus_rotation high-leakage soundness <=0.0208 under bounded noise, while projection_rotation is margin-sensitive and calibration side-channel leakage is rejected; Qiskit/Aer verifier bridge instantiates 216 ideal randomized parity-verifier circuits with 0 semantic mismatches, honest completeness 1.0, and up to 30 qubits including verifier ancillas; noisy Aer verifier bridge executes 9600 randomized parity-verifier circuits with honest and four adversary input families, bridge-safe noisy honest acceptance 1.0, bridge-safe noisy adversary acceptance 0.0, and at least 7 unknown independent predicates; backend-calibrated-style verifier bridge derives noise from Qiskit GenericBackendV2 target InstructionProperties and executes 5760 circuits with safe calibrated honest acceptance 1.0, safe calibrated adversary acceptance 0.25, max honest predicate-bit error 0.0703125, and no-refresh marked unsafe.
